# Casual Sheets — Excel-flavored web spreadsheet

> Open-source, self-hostable web spreadsheet that opens and saves native
> .xlsx (plus .ods, .csv, .tsv) with real-time multi-user co-editing.
> Behaves like Microsoft Excel — ribbon, formula bar, file-centric
> workflow. Built on Univer OSS (Apache-2.0) with a custom Office-style
> shell. Apache-2.0 throughout.

## Quick facts

- **License:** Apache-2.0
- **Repo:** https://github.com/schnsrw/sheets
- **Live demo:** https://sheet.schnsrw.live/
- **Docker:** https://hub.docker.com/r/schnsrw/casual-sheets
- **Marketing site:** https://schnsrw.live/casual-sheets/
- **Author:** Sachin Sarwa — https://schnsrw.live/
- **Current version:** v0.1.0
- **Test coverage:** 357 Playwright end-to-end tests on every push

## What it does

- Opens and saves native `.xlsx`, `.ods`, `.csv`, `.tsv` files.
  ExcelJS-backed parser with a 41/41-probe lossiness audit on
  styles, formulas, number formats, hyperlinks, defined names,
  data validation, page setup, tables, freeze, hidden sheets,
  and workbook metadata.
- `.xlsm` files round-trip byte-equal — captures `xl/vbaProject.bin`
  before parse and re-injects post-export with `[Content_Types].xml`
  + workbook-rel patching. We never execute the VBA.
- Real-time co-editing via Yjs + Hocuspocus over a stateless Go-free
  Node WebSocket gateway. Anonymous rooms identified by URL.
  Optional password-protected rooms with SHA-256 client gate +
  server `onAuthenticate` enforcement for view-only roles.
- Pivot tables with multi-row compact layout and drill-down
  (Ctrl+Shift+D). Sparklines: line, column, win-loss.
- 8 chart types (line, column, bar, pie, donut, scatter, area,
  combo) with trendlines, date-axis detection, per-series colour
  overrides. ECharts overlay anchored to cell ranges.
- Conditional formatting, data validation, named ranges, comments,
  hyperlinks, freeze panes, hidden sheets, tab colours, dark mode.
- Version history side panel with auto-snapshots and one-click
  restore (single-user mode).
- Autosave to IndexedDB with restore banner on reload.
- 11-template home gallery (Personal / Work / Finance / Education)
  with hand-tuned data-driven thumbnail previews.

## Architecture

- **Frontend:** React 18 + Vite + TypeScript strict, Univer OSS
  for the grid + formula engine.
- **Collab:** Yjs + Hocuspocus over WebSocket. Server-side view-only
  enforcement via `onAuthenticate`.
- **xlsx I/O:** ExcelJS in Web Workers. JSZip for raw OOXML-part
  passthrough (today: VBA).
- **ods / csv / tsv:** `@e965/xlsx` in Web Workers.
- **Charts:** ECharts overlay anchored to cell ranges via a custom
  coordinate transform that respects zoom + frozen panes.
- **Persistence:** Redis optional in the Docker image, 7-day TTL on
  rooms. No DB. State lives in memory while a session is active.
- **Container:** Node 22 Alpine, multi-arch (amd64 + arm64). Single
  port (3000) serves the web app + Hocuspocus + Fastify together.

## Architectural principles

- **File-centric.** Native Office formats are the source of truth.
  Open, edit, save back. Never an export-only model.
- **Stateless backend.** No DB, no on-disk update log in the
  real-time service. The host owns persistence (WOPI in v1+, inline
  in v0).
- **No accounts, no lock-in.** Anonymous sessions identified by room
  URL. Self-host the Docker image and you own everything.
- **OSS dependencies, no Pro pinning.** Univer OSS only — we never
  reach for Univer's commercial layer for charts/pivots/print.

## Sibling projects

Part of a three-project suite all by Sachin Sarwa:

- **Casual Sheets** — this project — https://sheet.schnsrw.live/
- **Casual Editor** — Word-flavored web `.docx` editor —
  https://doc.schnsrw.live/ — https://github.com/schnsrw/docx
- **Casual Desktop** — Tauri binaries reusing the web cores —
  in progress.

## Tech stack

- **Frontend:** TypeScript · React · Vite · Univer OSS
- **Realtime:** Yjs · Hocuspocus · WebSocket
- **xlsx:** ExcelJS · JSZip · `@e965/xlsx`
- **Charts:** ECharts
- **Backend (single Node service):** Fastify · Hocuspocus
- **Quality:** Playwright (357 e2e tests) · ESLint · Prettier · TypeScript strict
- **Ops:** Docker · GitHub Actions · GitHub Pages (demo) · Docker Hub
